criteria.statement: Identify every technology the District uses that processes personal data and generates predictions, recommendations, classifications, rankings, or scores used to make, guide, or assist a decision about an individual.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy requires the district to identify and inventory every automated decision-making technology it uses that generates predictions, recommendations, classifications, rankings, or scores used to make, guide, or assist a decision about an individual.
criteria.evidence_required: An inventory/identification obligation naming automated or algorithmic systems.
criteria.measurement_method: Detect an inventory duty AND reference to automated/algorithmic decision systems.
criteria.revision: The District shall identify and maintain an inventory of every automated decision-making technology it uses to make, guide, or assist a decision about a student.

criteria.statement: For each ADMT, determine whether it is used to materially influence a consequential decision (a non-de-minimis factor affecting the outcome); if so it is a covered ADMT.
criteria.verify_note: AG may set presumptions/objective indicators for 'materially influence' — 6-1-1706(5)(b); track rulemaking.
criteria.pass_semantics: Built to the standard we expect the AG to set; subject to final rules.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy provides a method to determine which systems are 'covered' — i.e., materially influence a consequential decision (a non-de-minimis factor affecting the outcome).
criteria.measurement_method: Detect a materiality test tied to consequential decisions.
criteria.revision: For each system, the District shall determine whether it materially influences a consequential decision, and if so treat it as a covered system under this policy.

criteria.statement: Determine which District decisions fall in the covered domain 'education enrollment or an education opportunity,' applying the exclusions for routine academic administration/student support (IX), classroom personalization, and low-stakes/routine processes (I).
criteria.artifact: Scope determination keyed to decision type
criteria.verify_note: Live battleground; AG considerations Q IV.2.4 seeks education sector-specific guidance; commenters urge broad construction — build to broad reading.
criteria.pass_semantics: Defensible reading taken where commenters/AG framing disagree; flagged for reconciliation.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy scopes which education decisions are consequential (enrollment or an education opportunity) and distinguishes routine academic administration and classroom personalization that are excluded.
criteria.evidence_required: An education-domain scope statement with the routine-administration carve-out.
criteria.revision: Consequential decisions in education include enrollment and education opportunities such as placement, eligibility, and program access; routine academic administration and classroom personalization that do not materially influence such decisions are excluded.

criteria.statement: Flag SB 25-200 universal K-3 dyslexia screeners as candidate covered ADMT (they produce predictive scores/classifications about students), pending materially-influence analysis.
criteria.verify_note: Dual clock lands 2027-28; coverage turns on materially-influence.
criteria.pass_semantics: Built to the standard we expect the AG to set; subject to final rules.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy flags predictive screeners (including SB 25-200 / READ Act dyslexia screeners) as candidate covered systems pending a materiality analysis.
criteria.evidence_required: Named treatment of screeners/predictive assessments as candidate ADMT.
gap_labels: their treatment as candidate covered systems
criteria.revision: Universal screeners that produce predictive scores or classifications about students, including READ Act dyslexia screeners, shall be evaluated as candidate covered systems.

criteria.statement: Maintain the ADMT inventory as a current register that feeds recordkeeping and re-screens on developer material updates.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy requires the inventory to be maintained as a current register and re-reviewed on developer material updates.
criteria.evidence_required: An ongoing-maintenance / update obligation for the inventory.
criteria.revision: The District shall keep the inventory current and re-review each system upon notice of a material update or modification from its developer.

criteria.statement: For each covered ADMT, classify the decision type and the covered domain it touches.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy classifies each covered system by decision type and covered domain.
criteria.measurement_method: Detect classification tied to decision type/domain.
gap_labels: classification by decision type or domain
criteria.revision: For each covered system, the District shall classify the decision type and the covered domain it affects.

criteria.statement: Confirm the District is subject to FERPA (it will be), which conditions the availability of the Part-17 FERPA safe harbors.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy establishes that the district is subject to FERPA, conditioning the Part-17 FERPA safe harbors.
criteria.revision: The District is an educational agency subject to the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA); FERPA processes are used to satisfy the notice and correction duties of this policy where available.

criteria.statement: Determine, per covered ADMT, whether notice/disclosure can be satisfied through the District's FERPA notices and student-record access procedures.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy determines, per covered system, whether notice/disclosure is satisfied through the district's FERPA notices and student-record access procedures.
criteria.evidence_required: A per-system FERPA notice safe-harbor determination.
criteria.measurement_method: Detect FERPA-notice routing tied to systems.
gap_labels: their use to satisfy ADMT notice
criteria.revision: For each covered system, the District shall determine whether the notice and disclosure duties are satisfied through its existing FERPA notices and student-record access procedures.

criteria.statement: Determine, per covered ADMT, whether correction and human review can be satisfied through existing student-record inspection/review/amendment and district appeal processes.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy determines, per covered system, whether correction and human review are satisfied through existing student-record inspection/review/amendment and district appeal processes.
criteria.evidence_required: A per-system FERPA rights safe-harbor determination.
criteria.revision: For each covered system, correction and human-review duties shall be satisfied through the District's student-record inspection, review, and amendment procedures and its complaint or appeal process.

criteria.statement: Confirm each vendor ADMT fits the FERPA school-official exception: legitimate educational interest, institutional function, district direct control, and use/re-disclosure limits.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy requires each vendor system to fit the FERPA school-official exception: legitimate educational interest, institutional function, district direct control, and use/re-disclosure limits.
criteria.revision: Each vendor system that accesses education records shall qualify under the FERPA school-official exception, with a legitimate educational interest, the District's direct control over records, and binding use and re-disclosure limits.

criteria.statement: Confirm each vendor qualifies and is bound as a 'school service contract provider' under the Student Data Transparency and Security Act (transparency, use limits, security, destruction).
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy binds vendors as 'school service contract providers' under the Colorado Student Data Transparency and Security Act (C.R.S. 22-16).
gap_labels: binding vendors as school service contract providers
criteria.revision: Vendors handling student data shall be bound as school service contract providers under the Colorado Student Data Transparency and Security Act (C.R.S. 22-16).

criteria.statement: Provide clear-and-conspicuous notice before using a covered ADMT to materially influence a consequential decision, with instructions to obtain further information.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy requires clear and conspicuous notice before a covered system is used to materially influence a consequential decision.
criteria.revision: Before a covered system is used to materially influence a consequential decision affecting a student, the District shall provide clear and conspicuous notice to the affected consumer.

criteria.statement: May satisfy the notice duty via a prominent public notice reasonably accessible/proximate at points of consumer interaction.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy allows compliance via a prominent public notice reasonably accessible at points of interaction.
criteria.revision: The District may satisfy the notice duty through a prominent public notice reasonably accessible at points of consumer interaction.

criteria.statement: Within 30 days of an adverse-outcome decision, provide a plain-language description, the ADMT's role, a process to request information (name, version, developer, data categories), and an explanation of consumer rights.
criteria.verify_note: Content further shaped by AG rules due 1/1/2027 — 6-1-1704(4); AG considerations Q IV.2.
criteria.pass_semantics: Built to the standard we expect the AG to set; subject to final rules.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy requires, within 30 days of an adverse outcome, a plain-language description, the system's role, a process to request information, and an explanation of consumer rights.
criteria.evidence_required: A 30-day post-adverse-outcome disclosure with the required elements.
criteria.revision: Within thirty days after an adverse outcome materially influenced by a covered system, the District shall provide a plain-language description of the decision, the system's role, a simple process to request further information, and an explanation of the consumer's rights.

criteria.statement: Where FERPA-subject, deliver notice/disclosure through FERPA notices and student-record access procedures (parent/guardian/eligible student); no separate process required.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy routes notice and disclosure through FERPA notices and student-record access procedures.
gap_labels: their use for notice and disclosure
criteria.revision: Notice and post-adverse-outcome disclosures shall be delivered through the District's FERPA notices and student-record access procedures, including notice to a parent, guardian, or eligible student.

criteria.statement: Provide notices/disclosures reasonably accessible to consumers with disabilities and limited English proficiency.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy makes notices reasonably accessible to consumers with disabilities and limited English proficiency.
criteria.revision: Notices and disclosures shall be reasonably accessible to consumers with disabilities and available to consumers with limited English proficiency in a language they understand.

criteria.statement: Provide parent/guardian/eligible-student access to, and a mechanism to request correction of, personal data, consistent with FERPA and the 22-16 parent-rights/complaint provisions.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy provides parent/guardian/eligible-student access to, and a mechanism to request correction of, personal data.
criteria.revision: A parent, guardian, or eligible student may access the personal data used in a decision and request correction of materially inaccurate data through the District's established process.

criteria.statement: Retain, for at least three years after each consequential decision, records reasonably necessary to demonstrate Part-17 compliance. No FERPA safe harbor applies to this duty.
criteria.verify_note: Standalone duty — the hardest; no FERPA analog.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy requires retaining records for at least three years after each consequential decision.
criteria.evidence_required: A 3-year (or longer) retention obligation tied to decisions.
criteria.revision: The District shall retain, for at least three years after each consequential decision, records reasonably necessary to demonstrate compliance with this policy.

criteria.statement: Records include covered-ADMT version identifiers, changelogs, and documentation of material mitigation changes.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy specifies records include system version identifiers, changelogs, and documentation of material mitigation changes.
criteria.revision: Retained records shall include covered-system version identifiers, changelogs, and documentation of material mitigation changes.

criteria.statement: Maintain a data-security policy and vendor data-destruction practices consistent with the Student Data Transparency and Security Act.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy maintains a data-security policy and vendor data-destruction consistent with C.R.S. 22-16.
criteria.revision: The District shall maintain a data-security policy and require vendor data destruction consistent with the Colorado Student Data Transparency and Security Act.

criteria.statement: Ensure records are sufficient to demonstrate compliance to the Attorney General (sole enforcer).
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy ensures records are sufficient to demonstrate compliance to the Attorney General.
criteria.revision: Records shall be organized and sufficient to demonstrate compliance to the Attorney General upon request.

criteria.statement: Obtain and retain developer documentation: intended and known-harmful uses, training-data categories (to the extent known), known limitations/risks, and appropriate-use/monitoring/human-review instructions.
criteria.verify_note: Developer duty; deployer must secure receipt (1702(1)(e) withholding notice).
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy requires obtaining developer documentation: intended and known-harmful uses, training-data categories, known limitations, and use/monitoring/human-review instructions.
criteria.revision: Before deploying a covered system, the District shall obtain the developer's documentation of intended and known-harmful uses, training-data categories, known limitations, and instructions for appropriate use, monitoring, and human review.

criteria.statement: Vendor contract imposes District direct control over records and purpose-limitation / use-&-re-disclosure limits (no reuse or resale).
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Vendor contracts impose district direct control and purpose-limitation / no-reuse / no-resale on records.
criteria.revision: Vendor contracts shall place education records under the District's direct control and limit their use to the contracted purpose, prohibiting reuse, resale, or re-disclosure.

criteria.statement: Vendor contract meets school-service-contract-provider transparency, use-limitation, security, and destruction duties.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Vendor contracts meet 22-16 transparency, use-limitation, security, and destruction duties.
criteria.revision: Vendor contracts shall satisfy the transparency, use-limitation, security, and destruction duties required of school service contract providers under C.R.S. 22-16.

criteria.statement: Procurement gate screens dyslexia/predictive screeners against the §1209(2.5) technical criteria (validity, classification accuracy, public technical manual, research-based cutoff points).
criteria.verify_note: Also supports construct-validity concerns raised by commenters.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Procurement screens predictive screeners against the READ Act §1209(2.5) technical criteria (validity, classification accuracy, public technical manual, cutoff points).
criteria.revision: Before adopting a screener or predictive assessment, the District shall verify it meets the READ Act technical criteria, including validity, classification accuracy, and a public technical manual with research-based cutoff points.

criteria.statement: Ensure contracts do not purport to indemnify against Colorado Anti-Discrimination Act liability arising from covered-ADMT decisions (such terms are void as against public policy).
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Contracts do not indemnify against Colorado Anti-Discrimination Act liability from covered-system decisions.
criteria.revision: No contract shall indemnify, defend, or hold harmless a party against liability under the Colorado Anti-Discrimination Act arising from a covered-system decision; such terms are void as against public policy.

criteria.statement: Process developer notices of material updates and modifications; trigger re-inventory and re-classification.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy processes developer notices of material updates and triggers re-inventory and re-classification.
criteria.revision: On receiving a developer notice of a material update or modification, the District shall re-inventory and re-classify the affected system.

criteria.statement: Designate a human reviewer with authority to approve, modify, or override the consequential decision.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy designates a trained human reviewer with explicit authority to approve, modify, or override a consequential decision materially influenced by a covered system, who does not default to the output.
criteria.evidence_required: A reviewer with named override authority, training, and non-deference.
criteria.measurement_method: Detect reviewer + override authority + training + non-default.
gap_labels: authority to approve, modify, or override
criteria.revision: A consequential decision materially influenced by a covered system shall be reviewed by a designated, trained District official who has authority to approve, modify, or override the decision and who does not default to the system's output.

criteria.statement: The reviewer considers relevant available primary evidence, is trained to conduct the review, and does not default to the system output.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy requires the reviewer to consider primary evidence, be trained, and not default to the system output.
criteria.revision: The reviewer shall consider the relevant available primary evidence, be trained to conduct the review, and shall not default to the system's output.

criteria.statement: The reviewer has access to understand the output's intended use, material limitations, input categories, and principal factors, without trade-secret disclosure.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy gives the reviewer access to understand the system's intended use, material limitations, input categories, and principal factors.
criteria.revision: The reviewer shall have access to information sufficient to understand the system's intended use, material limitations, categories of inputs, and the principal factors used to generate its output.

criteria.statement: On an adverse outcome, provide an opportunity for meaningful human review and reconsideration, to the extent commercially reasonable.
criteria.verify_note: 'Commercially reasonable' shaped by AG rules due 1/1/2027 — 6-1-1705(3); commenters propose proportionality factors.
criteria.pass_semantics: Built to the standard we expect the AG to set; subject to final rules.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy provides, on an adverse outcome, an opportunity for meaningful human review and reconsideration to the extent commercially reasonable.
criteria.revision: On an adverse outcome, the affected consumer shall have an opportunity for meaningful human review and reconsideration of the decision, to the extent commercially reasonable.

criteria.statement: Provide correction of factually incorrect or materially inaccurate personal data; correction of opinions, predictions, scores, or protected evaluations is not required.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy provides correction of factually inaccurate personal data (not opinions, predictions, scores, or protected evaluations).
criteria.evidence_required: A data-correction right with the evaluation carve-out.
gap_labels: its focus on factually inaccurate data
criteria.revision: A consumer may request correction of factually incorrect or materially inaccurate personal data used in a decision; correction of opinions, predictions, scores, or protected evaluations is not required.

criteria.statement: Where FERPA-subject, satisfy correction and human review through existing student-record inspection/review/amendment and district complaint/appeal processes.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy routes correction and human review through FERPA amendment and district complaint/appeal processes.
criteria.measurement_method: Detect FERPA amendment/appeal routing for review.
gap_labels: their use for correction and review
criteria.revision: Correction and human-review requests shall be handled through the District's FERPA student-record amendment and complaint or appeal processes.

criteria.statement: Document internal audits evidencing that reviewers do not default to the system output (e.g., a planted-error catch-rate program), anticipating the AG's 'meaningful review' standard.
criteria.verify_note: Differentiator; AG considerations consumer-rights Q1; Substrate Rec 2; Brown. Not yet required — provisional.
criteria.pass_semantics: Defensible reading taken where commenters/AG framing disagree; flagged for reconciliation.
criteria.pass_condition: Policy documents internal audits (e.g., a planted-error catch-rate program) evidencing that reviewers do not default to the system output.
criteria.revision: The District shall periodically audit its human-review process — for example, by measuring how often reviewers catch known errors — to demonstrate that reviewers do not default to the system's output.

criteria.statement: Part 17 is enforced exclusively by the Attorney General; it creates no new private right of action.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.

criteria.statement: A 60-day cure follows a notice of violation unless the violation is knowing or repeated; the cure provision repeals 1/1/2030.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.

criteria.statement: Existing Colorado Anti-Discrimination Act remedies (which carry private enforcement) are preserved; covered-ADMT disparate impact is the residual private-litigation channel.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.

criteria.statement: Effective 1/1/2027; applies to consequential decisions made on or after that date.
criteria.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
